name: Gold
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; covers the 7 June cutoff -> early 10 June; 8/9 June runs empty). **GOLD FELL 2.1% DURING A THREE-COUNTRY MISSILE STRIKE — THE WAR-RISK COMPLEX DID NOT BID THROUGH THE MAXIMUM KINETIC ESCALATION OF THE POST-APRIL WAR. Gold $4,168.90 (-2.1% 1D, -11.7% 1M, -20.3% 3M, lowest since ~23 March).** On June 9 gold FELL 2.1% DURING a three-country Iranian missile strike on US-allied capitals — it is now ~$170 below the pre-rout June 5 close and moving AWAY from the $4,600 regime trigger (now ~$430 away, the WRONG direction). The mechanism is unambiguous and is the cycle's cleanest inversion: oil-fed inflation + a hot May jobs print -> a FED-HIKE repricing (December hike to ~70% from ~45% a week ago) -> real yields + DXY (~99.9) up -> zero-yield gold repriced down. THIS IS AN INFLATION / REAL-YIELDS EVENT, NOT RISK-OFF — gold is falling BECAUSE of the Iran shock (shock -> oil -> inflation -> yields/dollar), not despite it. The regime flips ONLY on a MUTATION from 'inflation risk' to 'financial-system risk'; absent that, the Iran war is gold-NEGATIVE. The complex is deleveraging, not signalling demand: Silver $64.33 (-24.7% 1M), Platinum $1,679 (-20.6% 1M), Palladium $1,238 (+2.1% 1D / -18.2% 1M) — while copper resilient ($6.27/lb, -2.2% 1M) is the control variable proving this is a precious/leveraged-positioning unwind, not a growth scare. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS watching, now ~$430 below and moving AWAY (wrong direction). The opposite 'sub-$4,400 sustained 5+ sessions' watch is well tripped but is a RATES-driven move (jobs/oil -> hike-repricing), NOT a crisis-regime change — keep watching, do not over-encode. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **THE DEFINITIVE PROOF THIS IS A RATES EVENT, NOT A WAR RISK-OFF — GOLD FELL 3.1% TO A 2026 LOW ON A DAY VIX ROSE ~40%. A HOT JOBS REPORT KILLED FED-CUT HOPES AND RE-PRICED A QUARTER-POINT HIKE; THE ENTIRE NON-YIELDING METALS COMPLEX SOLD OFF.** Gold collapsed -3.1% 1D to $4,337.10 (intraday $4,481 -> $4,311; first sustained break below $4,370, a fresh 2026 low; -7.4% MoM, -15.7% 3M). The driver was unambiguously rates, not crisis: a HOT May jobs report (172K vs ~85K expected) killed Fed-cut hopes and priced a quarter-point HIKE by year-end -> US 10Y to 4.54% + DXY +0.7% -> the real-yield opportunity cost of zero-yield gold rose -> the whole metals complex sold off (Silver $68.94 -6.6%, Platinum $1,792 -5.4%, Palladium $1,247.10 -5.4%, Copper $6.26/lb -3.8%, Aluminum $3,950.25/mt -1.2%). **The single cleanest regime read of the cycle: gold FALLING 3% while VIX rose ~40% is the textbook 'rates-de-risk, not war-risk-off' signature** — in a war risk-off gold and VIX rise together; here they diverged violently. The same rates shock that killed gold also unwound the crowded AI/leveraged-Asia trade (Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + the jobs print = a broad 'sell-everything-rate-sensitive' day). Silver/PGM/Copper took the real-yield hit PLUS an industrial/growth-scare and a deleveraging cascade. **Gold remains NOT this cycle's crisis hedge — and it is now FURTHER below the $4,600 regime-shift trigger than at any point this cycle.** TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 6): 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' stays watching, now far below at $4,337. The old 'gold below $4,400 sustained 5+ sessions = second-leg premium unwind' watch is again sub-$4,400 (Day 1) — but this is a RATES-driven move (jobs -> hike-repricing), NOT a crisis-regime change; keep watching, do not over-encode. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE REGIME SELF-CONFIRMED — METALS STABILISED AS OIL EASED; GOLD A NON-MOVER (-0.2%) THAT DID NOT CATCH A HAVEN BID ON A TECH-LED RISK-OFF. THE MIRROR-IMAGE OF 3 JUNE.** With Brent down ~1% (less inflation impulse), the 3 June metals rout did NOT extend: Gold ~$4,467.80 (-0.2%), Silver $72.15 (modest intraday gains), both STABILISING rather than gapping — the mirror image of 3 June's -2 to -3.5% rout, and a clean confirmation that the driver is the oil->yields->dollar INFLATION channel, NOT risk-off. The mechanism: 3 June oil UP -> inflation/Fed-hike fear -> 10Y + dollar UP -> whole metals complex DOWN; 4 June oil DOWN ~1% -> less inflation impulse -> metals STOP falling — exactly what the regime predicts. Why gold didn't catch a haven bid on the tech sell-off (Broadcom): the risk-off was AI-SECTOR-SPECIFIC, not systemic; US 10Y at 4.49% (Fed expected to HIKE >=25bps by year-end, CME FedWatch) keeps the real-yield opportunity cost of zero-yield gold elevated; safe-haven flow still prefers the dollar (DXY held its weekly gains). **Gold remains NOT this cycle's crisis hedge.** The complacency tell: VIX printed a 15-handle (15.40, -4.1%) on a day with a rejected Lebanon ceasefire + two more battlefield deaths — MORE complacent than 3 June's 16.04; the tape is priced for the calibration to hold, and a non-calibrated act (US/allied-military deaths, an operational Hormuz closure) is NOT in this price — on that event gold/silver gap UP and the whole chain inverts. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 4)**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS far BELOW threshold ($4,467.80); no trigger moves. The regime flips ONLY if the crisis mutates from inflation/oil risk to financial-system risk. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **GOLD FELL -0.5% ON THE CYCLE'S FIRST CASUALTY DAY, AND THE WHOLE METALS COMPLEX SOLD OFF HARD — THE SINGLE CLEANEST 'INFLATION-EVENT-NOT-RISK-OFF' REGIME READ OF THE CYCLE.** On a casualty-producing US-Iran escalation day (first deaths, an Indian civilian killed at Kuwait airport), the classic prior is gold +1-2%. Instead Gold gave -0.5% to $4,465.90, and the rest of the complex fell HARDER: Platinum -3.5%, Palladium -3.0%, Silver -2.4%, Copper -2.3%. This is NOT a safe-haven failure — it is the market pricing the crisis as an INFLATION event: oil up -> US inflation already hot -> markets now price a Fed HIKE before year-end (INN/FXEmpire 'hawkish rate signals') -> US 10Y +0.9% to 4.49% + DXY +0.3% to 99.53 -> higher real yields + a stronger dollar crush non-yielding metals. Gold is the RELATIVE outperformer within metals (-0.5% vs silver -2.4%) because its safe-haven bid partially offsets the real-yield drag the PGMs lack — but it still could not print green on a day with the cycle's first deaths. Copper carries an extra demand-destruction signal (oil-inflation -> Fed restrictive -> growth fear). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 3)**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS BELOW threshold ($4,465.90); no trigger moves. The regime flips ONLY if the crisis mutates from 'inflation risk' to 'financial-system risk' (a Gulf-exposed bank wobble, a sovereign stress, a non-calibrated strike) — then gold and silver gap UP and this whole chain reverses. A gold pop on a FUTURE escalation day is the tell that the read has flipped. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **GOLD STILL DIDN'T BID THE KINETIC EXCHANGE — THE NON-MOVER, AGAIN; THE HIGHEST-CONVICTION REGIME CONFIRMATION OF THE CYCLE. On a day with a real US-Iran missile exchange (US strikes Goruk/Qeshm, MQ-1 shootdown, two Iranian missiles at Kuwait) + a 'complete Hormuz closure' threat, the classic prior is gold +1-3%. Gold gave +0.9% to $4,514 — and even THAT was the WRONG mechanism: it 'climbed back above $4,500 as a pullback in oil prices helped temper inflation concerns' (i.e. it rose on oil-down/real-yield relief, NOT safe-haven demand), having FALLEN the prior session. WHY: US 10Y at ~4.46-4.50% (Fed cuts fully priced out, hikes being priced post the 3.8% PCE) keeps the real-yield opportunity cost of zero-yield gold elevated; safe-haven flow keeps going into the DOLLAR, not gold. **This is the cleanest confirmation yet of the cycle-long regime call: the market is pricing an INFLATION/OIL event, not a financial-system-risk event — gold is NOT this cycle's crisis hedge.** The regime flips ONLY if the crisis mutates to financial-system risk (a Gulf-exposed bank wobble). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 2)**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS BELOW threshold ($4,514). No trigger moves. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **REVERSAL — GOLD DID NOT STAY BELOW $4,400; THE 'HEDGE IS DEAD / SUB-$4,400 WATCH' CALL FROM 28 MAY DID NOT SUSTAIN. Gold's 28 May break below $4,400 (two-month low ~$4,380) REVERSED WITHIN A SESSION: +2.56% to $4,524 on 29 May (Fortune; +$113 from the prior $4,411), and ~$4,560 (CNBC/markets, +1.4% 1D); it is ~flat-to-slightly-negative on the month (~-0.7% to +0.3%) and +36% YoY. The sub-$4,400 dip lasted ONE session — so the 28 May 'gold below $4,400 sustained 5+ sessions = second-leg premium unwind (Day 1)' watch is marked UNTRIPPED/RESET, not activating. The driver was the SOFTER monthly PCE (April monthly +0.4% vs +0.5% expected, released 28 May) dialing back the Fed rate-HIKE bets that had crushed gold, plus a softer dollar (DXY 98.91) — note gold ROSE on de-escalation headlines (counter to the simple 'ceasefire = sell gold' read), so the dollar-weakness/inflation-hedge bid reasserted. **The cycle-long regime read HOLDS: gold trades on REAL YIELDS and the FED, not on the crisis.** The $4,600 regime-shift trigger remains BELOW threshold. Do NOT encode a one-day low as a regime change in either direction.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **GOLD BROKE $4,400 TO A TWO-MONTH LOW ON A DAY WITH A LIVE US-IRAN MISSILE EXCHANGE — THE HIGHEST-CONVICTION NON-MOVER SIGNAL IN THE DATASET. Spot gold FELL 1.7% to $4,380.62 (its lowest since March 26, breaking below $4,400), ~$4,450 Thursday — DOWN over two sessions. DATA-VINTAGE FLAG: yfinance prints $4,540 / +2.1% — a STALE-COMPARATOR ARTIFACT (gold actually FELL ~2%); do NOT encode +2.1%.** On a day when the IRGC fired a ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (A1) and Israel's Lebanon ground op continued, the classic prior is gold +1-3%. Instead gold FELL and the dollar rose to a one-week high. WHY (crisis-INVERSE mechanism): rising oil (Brent +2.4% intraday) → heightened inflation worries → markets price OUT Fed cuts and price IN hikes (April core PCE 3.3% released today) → US 10Y firm + dollar firm → real-yield opportunity cost of zero-yield gold rises → gold sold off; safe-haven flow on the Iranian strike went INTO THE DOLLAR, not gold (CNBC: 'Gold falls as rising oil prices boost rate hike bets; dollar, yields rise'). **This is the cleanest confirmation yet of the cycle-long regime call: the market is pricing an INFLATION/OIL event, NOT a financial-system-risk event — gold is NOT this cycle's crisis hedge. Gold failing to bid on a literal missile strike on US forces is the highest-conviction non-mover signal in the dataset; it failed HARDER than the May 27 'flat' test (it didn't just fail to bid — it fell to a two-month low).** Trigger 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS watching, far below. Trigger 'Gold below $4,400 sustained 5+ sessions = second-leg premium unwind' now ACTIVATING — Day 1 (broke $4,400 May 27-28). The regime flips ONLY if the crisis mutates from 'inflation/oil risk' to FINANCIAL-SYSTEM risk (a Gulf-exposed bank in distress, a sovereign wobble). Silver flat-to-down (tracks gold; yfinance +2.1% suspect). PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27). **GOLD DID NOT BID THE STRIKES — ~$4,481-4,523, -0.4% 1D, -4.2% MoM, -14.3% 3M, AND STILL FAR BELOW THE $4,600 REGIME-SHIFT TRIGGER. The week delivered the textbook safe-haven setup — US self-defense strikes inside southern Iran (intel A1), Israel's expanded Lebanon ground op across the Litani (intel A3), an oil whipsaw — and gold gave -0.4%, essentially flat. WHY: the safe-haven flow went into the DOLLAR, not gold (DXY firm at 99.22), and US 10Y at 4.48% (gold pays no yield) keeps the real-yield opportunity cost elevated; gold, silver, and copper all sold off together on rising yields + a firming dollar. THIS CONFIRMS THE CYCLE-LONG READ: the market is treating the crisis as a deal/de-escalation + inflation-via-oil event, NOT a financial-system-risk event, so gold is NOT this cycle's crisis hedge. The regime flips ONLY if the crisis mutates from 'inflation/oil risk' to 'financial-system risk' (a Gulf-exposed bank in distress, a sovereign wobble) — then gold breaks $4,600 and the relationship flips. Silver -2.1% 1D / -19.4% 3M is the same story amplified by its industrial beta. Trigger 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' STAYS watching, BELOW threshold.** PRIOR (May 25): Day 87 of Hormuz blockade (Monday May 25 — US Memorial Day + UK Spring Bank Holiday; COMEX/LME CLOSED, NO LIVE MONDAY GOLD READ). **GOLD STILL CARRIES THE FROZEN FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE OF $4,523 (-4.2% MoM, -13.1% 3M) — THERE IS NO LIVE MONDAY PRINT; DO NOT FABRICATE A MONDAY MOVE. Tuesday May 26 is the first real US read. On the LIVE-traded clean-room session, the deal-anticipation trade ran hard (Brent <$100 / WTI ~$91, India +1.3%, rupee firmer, India VIX -7.9%) — and gold, frozen, contributed NOTHING to the risk-off side, which is itself consistent with the cycle's 'underperformer / inflation-real-yield regime, not financial-system-risk regime' read. The 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift' trigger remains BELOW threshold at the frozen $4,523 — no progress possible until COMEX reopens Tuesday. Cross-asset note (B2): the rial firmed to ~1.71m/USD on deal anticipation, away from the 2,000,000 hyperinflation trigger — consistent with oil down and the deal-anticipation tape, and a marginal headwind for any gold safe-haven bid. The regime-shift catalysts (Gulf-exposed bank distress, Iran sovereign default, US-IRGC kinetic exchange closing Hormuz >30 days) remain absent; if the deal-fails / kinetic path re-opens, gold can still spike +$200-500 in 2-3 sessions.** PRIOR (May 24): **GOLD $4,523 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (-0.4% 1D, -3.9% MoM, -12.3% 3M) — THE CYCLE UNDERPERFORMER. GOLD'S FAILURE TO BID FOR 86 STRAIGHT DAYS OF AN ACTIVE HORMUZ BLOCKADE IS THE MOST IMPORTANT NON-CONFIRMATION SIGNAL IN THE DATA. CRISIS-HEDGE FUNCTION BROKEN (OR ALREADY-DISCOUNTED; OR REGIME-SHIFTED). EITHER: (a) THE MARKET BELIEVES THE DEAL WILL HAPPEN AND IS UNWINDING CRISIS-HEDGES PROACTIVELY, OR (b) THE INFLATION/REAL-YIELD REGIME HAS STRUCTURALLY REPRESSED GOLD'S RISK-OFF FUNCTION, OR (c) GOLD IS MISPRICED AND A REGIME-SHIFT CATALYST (DEAL FAILURE + BANK WITH GULF EXPOSURE IN DISTRESS + IRAN ESCALATION) SENDS IT +$300 QUICKLY. THE MONDAY ASIA OPEN IS THE FALSIFICATION TEST: IF BRENT GAPS $5-8 UP ON MONDAY ON DEAL-FADE AND GOLD STAYS SUB-$4,550, THEN (b) — REGIME CHANGE. IF GOLD +$50-80 SAME SCENARIO, THEN (a) — REPRICING. IF GOLD +$200+, THEN (c) — CATALYST-DRIVEN.** Per markets dossier C4: gold's underperformance explained by (1) US 10Y at 4.56% — real yields competing with gold (Fed not cutting fast → real yields stay positive → gold's opportunity cost remains elevated); (2) Dollar Index 99.32 firm — DXY +1.5% on 3M, safe-haven flow rotating INTO USD rather than into gold; (3) deal-anticipation suppressing risk-off bid — investors who would normally hedge with gold are buying VIX puts and equities directly; (4) no central-bank-buying surge announced this quarter — the structural EM-CB gold bid that drove 2024-2025 has paused. **NEW TRIGGER ADDED**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift confirmed (correlation pattern with systemic-crisis regime)' — Day 0 of 3, BELOW threshold at $4,523. **Silver $76.20** (-0.3% 1D, +1.0% MoM, -12.9% 3M); **Copper $6.38/lb** (+1.9% 1D, +5.0% MoM, +7.7% 3M) — non-crisis AI bid intact; **Platinum $1,939** (-0.8% 1D, -4.1% MoM, -11.1% 3M); **Palladium $1,360** (-1.7% 1D, -8.4% MoM, -25.0% 3M). PRIOR (May 23): GOLD $4,521 MAY 22 CLOSE (-0.4% 1D, -3.9% MoM, -13.1% 3M) — THE UNDERPERFORMER REGIME. THURSDAY MAY 21 BREAK TO $4,510.60 ON MOJTABA DIRECTIVE LEAK DID NOT FOLLOW THROUGH — DEAL-ANTICIPATION FLOW OUT OF GOLD DOMINATES THE DIRECTIVE-INFLOWS IN. THE FRIDAY FAILURE TO BID THE MAY 23-25 MEMORIAL DAY WEEKEND KINETIC WINDOW IS THE CLEANEST CROSS-ASSET SIGNAL THAT MARKETS DO NOT BELIEVE TRUMP PULLS THE TRIGGER.** Expected given: Mojtaba directive Day 2 of 14 (no Iranian state-media confirmation OR walk-back) + CBS Pentagon-source US military Memorial Day cancellation + Tasnim military source 'Iran preparing for resuming fighting' + Munir COAS Tehran visit + Trump '50/50' framing + Houthi Haifa rhetoric Day 5 of 14 + Israeli unilateral risk + Day 5 of 5 May 24 strike-window: gold should follow through Thursday's $4,500-break with Friday +1-2% bid. **Got**: flat-to-down. **Why underperforming**: (1) Deal-anticipation flow OUT of gold dominates — ETF flows turned negative this week per Trading Economics aggregator; (2) US 10Y at 4.56% provides real-yield competition (5.4% 1M move higher) — investors choose duration over gold; (3) DXY 99.32 stable-to-firm → dollar safe-haven bid competes; (4) COMEX positioning crowded at year-highs → less marginal buying capacity; (5) Mojtaba directive being priced as 'leak architecture, not actual policy' — market discounted its directness; (6) Trump's softer 'satisfactorily handled' uranium formulation signals US verbally accommodating directive's substance, reducing nuclear-collapse tail. **Mechanism reading**: the market is treating this crisis as an OIL-INFLATION event (bad for gold vs real yields) more than a FINANCIAL-SYSTEM-RISK event (good for gold). Gold is NOT pricing tail-risk because the deal track is being priced as DONE. **If a regime shift occurred — a bank with Gulf exposure in trouble, Iran sovereign default, US-IRGC kinetic exchange that closes Hormuz >30 days — gold spikes $200-500 to $4,800-5,000+ in 2-3 sessions.** **The Friday non-mover IS the intelligence.** **Trigger**: 'Gold sustains above $4,600 for 3+ sessions = regime shift confirmed' — Day 0 of 3, BELOW threshold ($4,521 < $4,600). **NEW WATCH**: If gold breaks above $4,580 on a Sunday-night Asia open or Monday open WITHOUT a kinetic event, the regime is shifting back to risk-off without clear catalyst — likely Pakistan-track diplomatic stall signal. **Silver $75.89** (-0.7% 1D, +0.6% MoM, -12.3% 3M) — tracking gold weakness. **Copper $6.34/lb** (+1.4% 1D, +4.4% MoM, +9.9% 3M) — non-crisis bid: AI-cycle demand + China stimulus optionality. **Platinum $1,931.60** (-1.2% 1D, -4.5% MoM, -10.0% 3M). **Palladium $1,358.00** (-1.8% 1D, -8.5% MoM, -23.4% 3M). **Aluminum $3,963.50/mt** — previous stale-comparator anomaly self-corrected, May 22 print reconciles with 1M/3M trend (+9.6% MoM, +31.5% 3M). Today, no such signal. **Reversibility**: Gold's -13.2% 3M is approximately 50% real-yield-channel (structural, doesn't reverse), 50% crisis-regime-shift (deal-track-driven). On Pakistan deal sign, gold could see further -$200 to ~$4,300.
